top_signals.title: World absorbs historic Iran war oil supply loss, but depleted stocks bring risks
top_signals.summary: Replenishing oil stocks, never cheap, has likely been made more expensive by the war.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Putin is running out of options
top_signals.summary: The Russian leader may want to escalate his war — but that is harder than it sounds

top_signals.title: Agents of change − speech by Sarah Breeden
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Spyware found on phone of European Parliament member probing it
top_signals.summary: Stelios Kouloglou, formerly a member of the European Parliament's committee investigating abuses of commercial spyware, was twice infected with Pegasus while serving, researchers said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: AI Trade Faces Big Test as Trump Rings Opening Bell | Open Interest 7/6/2026
top_signals.summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." It's a  pivotal week for the AI trade: SpaceX joins the Nasdaq 100, Samsung reports earnings, and SK Hynix eyes a US debut. Plus, President Trump rings the opening bell for Trump Accounts, Xbox cuts 3,200 jobs, and we talk about Wall Street’s next big sports play. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Supreme Court decision threatens EU-US data transfer agreement
top_signals.summary: In a Tuesday letter, Max Schrems, the founder of the Vienna-based privacy advocacy organization noyb, told European officials he plans to sue to invalidate the EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework (DPF) that allows for the transfer of personal data from the EU to U.S. companies.

alerts.title: Supreme Court decision threatens EU-US data transfer agreement
alerts.summary: In a Tuesday letter, Max Schrems, the founder of the Vienna-based privacy advocacy organization noyb, told European officials he plans to sue to invalidate the EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework (DPF) that allows for the transfer of personal data from the EU to U.S. companies.
